elements.name: Title
texts: "Those trackless hills enfold the lake as though to hide it."

elements.name: Description
texts: "It is remarkable how completely the exterior world is shut out. Those trackless hills enfold the lake as though to hide it..."

elements.name: Source
texts: Curtayne, Alice, Lough Derg: St. Patrick’s Purgatory, p. 2

elements.name: Publisher
texts: Burns Oats and Washbourn, Ltd., London and Dublin

elements.name: Rights
texts: Citation for the purposes of criticism

elements.name: Text
texts: "It is remarkable how completely the exterior world is shut out. Those trackless hills enfold the lake as though to hide it. The nearest railway station is four miles away. Despite the ancient and enduring interest that invests this place, it has never attracted any permanent dwellers. The barren soil seems to forbid the formation of homesteads on it."
